title: rosa rubiginosa var. aucheri
intro: Rosa rubiginosa var. aucheri, also known as the Wild Briar or Sweetbriar, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Turkey. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa var. aucheri include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea infusions and infusions of the flowers in hot water, as well as the use of its bark and roots in tinctures and decoctions.
